figures.caption: Fig. 6. Buck converter with a MOSFET model and a controlled voltage source replacing the voltage controlled resistor from Fig. 5. The subcircuit on the right shows the internals of the MOSFET model.

Fig. 3. Example illustrating (ground) node identification, where the branches b2,i with 1 ≤ i ≤ 3 again belong to case i in the proof, respectively. The nodes are also again ordered to comply with the notation from (22).
Fig. 5. Buck converter with simple voltage control, modeled by a voltage controlled resistor gS(vR).
Fig. 8. Voltage controlled switched-mode power supply model based on the buck converter from Fig. 6.
Fig. 4. Example illustrating the basis matrices V and W of an incidence matrix.
